repo_notes.content: SCOPE & OUTPUT CONTRACT: Generate EXACTLY three English-only pages — 'Skill Inventory', 'Technical Evidence', 'Quantifiables' — plus conditionally 'Dependency Graph' (include ONLY for repos with inter-process communication such as ROS nodes, microservices, or multi-process pipelines; omit for single-process libraries and note omission in Skill Inventory prose). Zero Chinese text. These pages are STRUCTURED RAW DATA for an automated extraction pipeline (Stage 2: Extractor). Do NOT generate resume bullets, accomplishment statements, or text intended for direct resume inclusion. Your job is EVIDENCE EXTRACTION, not resume writing. Every data element must participate in the traceability chain: taxonomy skill (S::) → evidence unit (E::) → quantifiable metric (M::). Do NOT extract data that cannot bind to at least one link in this chain.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: TAXONOMY BINDING: The file .devin/taxonomy.yaml defines 8 tracks (T1-T8) × 5 levels (L0-L4). Each level contains a 'skills' map of snake_case keys, each with an 'aliases' array. This taxonomy is the SOLE AUTHORITY for skill identity. Emit ONLY (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs that exist in the file. Format: taxonomy_ref = 'T{1-8}-L{0-4}', skill_name = snake_case key under that level. The workflow is SKILL-FIRST: iterate each taxonomy skill, search the codebase using its aliases, then record findings. Do NOT scan the codebase first and try to retrofit findings to the taxonomy. Technologies with no taxonomy match go under unmapped_skills with suggested (track, level) and rationale. Levels (L0-L4) serve as CLASSIFICATION tiers for organizing skills by complexity, not as proficiency truth claims.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: YAML FORMAT CONTRACT: Each page MUST contain exactly ONE primary fenced YAML code block (```yaml ... ```) as its machine-readable payload. Supplementary discussion goes OUTSIDE as Markdown prose. Requirements: (1) Parseable by strict YAML 1.2 parser. (2) 2-space indentation, no tabs. (3) Strings containing ':', '#', or leading special chars must be single-quoted. (4) No trailing commas. (5) Block MUST begin with schema_version, source_repo, generated_at, and _meta keys in that order. Stage 2 extracts the FIRST ```yaml``` fenced block per page and parses it — ensure it is self-contained and complete. Prose sections after the YAML block use Markdown ## headings.
repo_notes.author: szzhu
szzhu

repo_notes.content:
ID SCHEMA & CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY: Entity IDs encode (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) for direct mapping to bullet schema:
  S::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name} — Skill Inventory entries (e.g., S::T3-L3:gicp_fine_registration)
  E::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key} — Technical Evidence units (e.g., E::T3-L3:gicp_fine_registration:multi_stage_gicp)
  M::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{metric_key} — Quantifiable metrics (e.g., M::T3-L3:gicp_fine_registration:max_correspondence_distance)
  P{nn} — Pipeline chains in Dependency Graph (P01, P02, ...) 
Forward refs (authority direction): Skill.evidence_refs → [E::...], Skill.metric_refs → [M::...], Evidence.metric_refs → [M::...].
Reverse refs (validation): Evidence.skill_refs → [S::...], Metric.evidence_refs → [E::...].
Integrity rules enforced by Stage 2: (1) Every S::... must appear in ≥1 E::....skill_refs. (2) Every E::... must appear in ≥1 S::....evidence_refs. (3) M::... refs are optional — not all evidence produces metrics. (4) No dangling references: every referenced ID must exist in its target page. (5) Integration-category evidence MUST have ≥2 entries in skill_refs. Stage 2 rejects pages with integrity violations and requests re-extraction.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
USAGE DEPTH CLASSIFICATION: Each skill receives a usage_depth enum imposing a CEILING on the claimable taxonomy level:
  'declared' → Ceiling L0 (dependency manifests only: package.xml, requirements.txt, find_package).
  'imported' → Ceiling L1 (import/include + default API calls, no customization).
  'configured' → Ceiling L2 (parameters tuned, config/launch files authored for this use case).
  'customized' → Ceiling L3 (source-level modifications — subclassed, extended, custom plugins, non-trivial algorithms).
  'architected' → Ceiling L4 (system designed around this tech — defined interfaces, made trade-offs, wrote abstractions consumed by others).
Ceiling check: parse the level digit from taxonomy_ref and compare against the ceiling. Example: T3-L3 + configured (ceiling L2) → FAIL. Each skill entry must include ceiling_check: 'PASS' or 'FAIL: L{n} exceeds {depth} ceiling L{n}'. When uncertain between two depths, ALWAYS classify DOWN.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL: (1) Cannot locate a file path → file: '[not found]', classification: 'declared', tag '[inferred]'. Stage 2 will attempt resolution. (2) Line numbers uncertain beyond ±20 → '~' prefix AND confidence: low. (3) Uncertain between usage depths → choose LOWER. (4) NEVER round metrics: extract EXACT values (47.3 stays 47.3, not '~50'). (5) README performance claims without code/test backing → source_type: 'claimed', flag: '[unverified]'. (6) Fewer than 5 extractable metrics is acceptable — do NOT pad with estimates. Quality over quantity. (7) Do NOT infer skill relationships without shared code regions as evidence. (8) Derived values → flag: '[derived]', derivation field MUST show the formula.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
PROVENANCE METADATA: Every page's YAML block MUST begin with these top-level keys in order:
  schema_version: '3.0'
  source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
  generated_at: '<ISO-8601 timestamp>'
  _meta:
    files_scanned: <int>
    files_in_repo: <int>
    scan_coverage_pct: <float 0-100>
    directories_skipped: ['<path>']
    extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
    confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
    known_gaps: ['<description>']
Stage 2 triggers re-extraction when: scan_coverage_pct < 60 OR extraction_confidence = 'low' OR len(known_gaps) > 3.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
EVIDENCE DENSITY & CONSOLIDATION: Governs output volume to prevent thin, repetitive entries that waste output budget and degrade Stage 2 parsing quality.

(1) PROFICIENCY EVIDENCE CAP: Each matched_skill in Skill Inventory carries AT MOST 3 proficiency_evidence citations, ranked by strength. Ranking: classification (implemented > configured > tested > declared), then confidence (high > medium > low), then subsystem diversity (prefer evidence from different components over repeated hits in the same file). The file_count field reports total files where the skill was detected — always ≥ len(proficiency_evidence).

(2) EVIDENCE UNIT CONSOLIDATION: When the same pattern or capability appears across N files (e.g., ROS topic publishing in 12 files), create ONE evidence unit with 1-2 strongest citations and note 'Pattern observed across N files.' Create separate units ONLY for genuinely different techniques, algorithms, or integration patterns. Consolidation test: 'Does this new unit teach Stage 3 something the existing unit does not?' If no → merge.

(3) TARGET DENSITY: evidence_units: 15-40 per repo. metrics: 5-25 per repo. pipeline_chains: 3-10 per repo. matched_skills: no artificial cap (extract all taxonomy matches). These are guidelines — quality always beats quantity.

(4) MINIMUM CONTENT THRESHOLD: evidence_unit.description ≥ 2 factual sentences. metric.context ≥ 10 words. pipeline_chain.description ≥ 1 sentence. Entries below threshold trigger Stage 2 warning and may be rejected.

(5) DEDUPLICATION: No two evidence units may describe the same fact about the same code region. No two metrics may capture the same (value, unit, context) triple. When in doubt, consolidate into one richer entry rather than two thin ones.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Skill Inventory
pages.purpose: For each (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pair in .devin/taxonomy.yaml, search the codebase for evidence using its aliases. Classify usage depth with ceiling checks, extract ATS keywords, and document file-level proficiency evidence (capped at 3 strongest per skill). Partition into matched_skills (taxonomy-bound) and unmapped_skills (observed but absent from taxonomy). Include tech_stack summary. This page ANCHORS the traceability chain — every downstream evidence unit and metric must link back to at least one skill entry here.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Skill Inventory:
```yaml
schema_version: '3.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

matched_skills:
  - id: 'S::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}'
    taxonomy_ref: 'T{n}-L{n}'
    skill_name: '<snake_case key from taxonomy>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    usage_depth_justification: '<≤3 factual sentences: specific code patterns that triggered this depth classification>'
    ceiling_check: '<PASS | FAIL: L{n} exceeds {depth} ceiling L{n}>'
    evidence_summary: '<≤3 factual sentences describing codebase usage of this skill>'
    file_count: <int>  # total files where skill was detected; always ≥ len(proficiency_evidence)
    proficiency_evidence:  # MAX 3 entries, ranked by evidence strength
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<start-end>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤20 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    ats_keywords:
      exact: ['<verbatim from taxonomy aliases, case-preserved>']
      semantic:
        - keyword: '<code-observed term NOT in aliases>'
          source_file: '<path where observed>'
    evidence_refs: ['E::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key}']  # forward refs to Technical Evidence; Must contain at least one evidence, support more
    metric_refs: ['M::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{metric_key}']  # forward refs to Quantifiables; [] if no metrics

unmapped_skills:
  - id: 'S::{suggested_track}-{suggested_level}:{skill_name}'  # skill_name = name lowercased, spaces→underscores; unique throughout the page
    name: '<technology name>'
    skill_name: '<snake_case version of name — spaces replaced by underscores, same pattern as taxonomy skill_name>'
    observed_in: '<file path>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    usage_depth_justification: '<≤3 factual sentences: specific code patterns that triggered this depth classification>'
    ceiling_check: '<PASS | FAIL: L{n} exceeds {depth} ceiling L{n}>'
    suggested_track: 'T{n}'
    suggested_level: 'L{n}'
    rationale: '<why this track and level>'
    suggested_evidence_refs: ['E::{suggested_track}-{suggested_level}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key}']  # forward refs to Technical Evidence
    suggested_metric_refs: ['M::{suggested_track}-{suggested_level}:{skill_name}:{metric_key}']  # forward refs to Quantifiables; [] if no metrics
    suggested_evidence_summary: '<≤3 factual sentences describing codebase usage of this skill>'

tech_stack:
  languages:
    - name: '<language>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      loc_pct: <float>
  frameworks:
    - name: '<framework>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      role: '<middleware|web|testing|build|etc>'
  build_tools:
    - name: '<tool>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
  testing:
    - framework: '<name>'
      test_count_est: <int>
```
After the YAML block, include Markdown prose sections:
## Architecture Context
2-3 paragraphs on how components compose into a working system. Identify major subsystems and their responsibilities.
## Skill Relationship Overview
Describe which skills co-occur in the same code regions, what integration patterns exist, and which files demonstrate the coupling. Reference integration evidence unit IDs (E::...) from the Technical Evidence page. This section is the PRIMARY guide for Stage 3 when composing cross-track resume bullets.
If this is a single-process library with no IPC, append: 'Dependency Graph page omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture'
page_notes.author: szzhu

page_notes.content:
ATS KEYWORD RULES: The 'aliases' arrays in taxonomy.yaml are the PRIMARY ATS keyword source. Partition into 'exact' (drawn VERBATIM from aliases, case-preserved) and 'semantic' (additional terms observed in code — class names, function names, API calls, topic names, message types — that are NOT already in the aliases array). Do NOT duplicate aliases into semantic. Each semantic keyword must cite its source_file.

PROFICIENCY EVIDENCE SELECTION: When a skill appears in more than 3 files, select the top 3 citations using this priority:
  (1) Classification strength: implemented > configured > tested > declared
  (2) Confidence level: high > medium > low
  (3) Specificity: prefer files demonstrating complex or customized usage over simple imports or declarations
  (4) Subsystem diversity: when evidence strength is equal, prefer citations from different components to show breadth
The file_count field captures total scope; proficiency_evidence captures best evidence. Do NOT list all files — that is what evidence_refs and Technical Evidence are for.

RELATIONSHIP DISCOVERY: Skill Inventory does NOT carry a formal relationship graph — integration structure lives in Technical Evidence as integration-category evidence units (category: integration, ≥2 skill_refs). However, the '## Skill Relationship Overview' prose MUST describe observed co-occurrences and name the relevant E{nn} IDs. Two skills are related ONLY if they interact in the same file or call chain — co-existing in the same repo without code-level interaction is NOT a relationship.
page_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Technical Evidence
pages.purpose: Extract atomic evidence units — factual statements about what the code does, how, and why — each with file-level citations. Categories: 'implementation' (algorithms, API usage, data structures), 'configuration' (parameter and launch choices), 'integration' (cross-component interaction, MUST reference ≥2 skills), 'architecture' (system-design decisions), 'testing' (test coverage and approach). Also extract project_context. Do NOT write resume bullets or accomplishment claims — extract FACTS that Stage 3 composes into bullets.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Technical Evidence:
```yaml
schema_version: '3.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

project_context:
  domain: '<application domain (e.g., robotics system, web platform, HAL, computer vision, deep learning, reinforcement learning, motion planning, manipulation, locomotion, etc.)>'
  problem_statement: '<≤3 factual sentences describing the problem the system solves>'
  system_overview: '<≤4 factual sentences describing the high-level system architecture>'
  scale_indicators:
    total_loc: <int>
    total_files: <int>
    primary_language: '<language>'
    secondary_languages: ['<language>']

evidence_units:
  - id: 'E::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key}'
    category: <implementation|configuration|integration|architecture|testing>
    title: '<≤10 word factual title>'
    description: '<≤6 factual sentences. State WHAT the code does, HOW (specific algorithm, pattern, or technique), and its PURPOSE in the system. No subjective adjectives.>'
    skill_refs: ['S::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}']  # reverse refs to Skill Inventory; integration MUST have ≥2
    citations:
      - file: '<repo-relative path>'
        lines: '<start-end>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    metric_refs: ['M::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{metric_key}']  # forward refs to Quantifiables; [] if no metrics
    tags: ['<optional categorical tags for Stage 3 grouping>']  # e.g., 'perception', 'control', 'communication', 'safety'
```
After the YAML block, include Markdown prose sections:
## Evidence Distribution
1 paragraph summarizing evidence count by category (e.g., '15 implementation, 5 configuration, 8 integration, 3 architecture, 2 testing'). Note categories with zero evidence.
## Integration Patterns
2-3 paragraphs describing cross-skill integration patterns found. Name specific E::... IDs. This section is the SECONDARY guide (after Skill Relationship Overview) for Stage 3 cross-track bullets.
page_notes.author: szzhu

page_notes.content:
EVIDENCE CATEGORIZATION RULES:
  'implementation': Algorithms, API usage, data structures, core computation logic. Minimum content: name the specific technique or algorithm AND its purpose in the system.
  'configuration': Parameter choices, launch/config file authorship, tuned values that affect system behavior. Minimum content: name the parameter AND explain why the chosen value matters or what behavior it controls.
  'integration': Cross-component interaction — data flow between modules, message passing, shared data structures, API calls across subsystems. MUST have ≥2 entries in skill_refs. Minimum content: name BOTH interacting skills AND describe the data or control flow between them.
  'architecture': System-design decisions — abstraction boundaries, interface definitions, design patterns, technology selection rationale, trade-offs. Minimum content: name the decision AND its rationale or trade-off.
  'testing': Test infrastructure, test cases, test coverage, testing methodology. Minimum content: name WHAT is tested AND the testing technique (unit, integration, simulation, hardware-in-loop, etc.).

CONSOLIDATION RULES:
  (1) Same capability across N files with identical pattern → ONE evidence unit, 1-2 strongest citations, note 'Pattern observed across N files.'
  (2) Different aspects of the same skill → SEPARATE units (e.g., 'VoxelGrid filtering' and 'RANSAC plane segmentation' are distinct implementation units even though both use PCL).
  (3) Consolidation test: 'Does this unit teach Stage 3 something new that no existing unit captures?' If no → merge into the richer existing unit.
  (4) Integration-category units are the MOST VALUABLE for resume generation — invest extra description detail here.

DESCRIPTION QUALITY GATE: Each description MUST contain: (a) the specific technique, algorithm, or pattern by name; (b) at least one concrete detail (parameter value, data type, library call, message type); (c) its role or purpose in the system context. Vague descriptions ('uses PCL for processing') will be flagged by Stage 2 — be specific ('Applies PCL VoxelGrid filter with adaptive leaf size computed from point cloud bounding box extents to downsample raw depth-camera output before registration pipeline ingestion').
page_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Quantifiables
pages.purpose: Extract ALL numbers, metrics, and quantifiable signals from the codebase. Source types: 'configured' (parameters, gains, rates, tolerances), 'counted' (files, functions, test cases, LOC per module), 'measured' (benchmarks, test results, profiled values), 'claimed' (README/docs assertions — always flag '[unverified]'). Extract raw values with units and source citations. Do NOT interpret or spin these numbers — Stage 3 decides which become resume metrics.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Quantifiables:
```yaml
schema_version: '3.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

metrics:
  - id: 'M::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{metric_key}'
    name: '<descriptive metric name (e.g., voxel_grid_leaf_size, ros_topic_count, registration_fitness_threshold)>'
    value: <exact raw value — never rounded>
    unit: '<unit (e.g., m, Hz, ms, count, pct, mm, σ, dimensionless)>'
    source_type: <configured|counted|measured|claimed>
    context: '<≤6 factual sentences: what this number represents in the system and why it matters>'
    evidence_refs: ['E::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key}']  # reverse refs to Technical Evidence units
    citation:
      file: '<repo-relative path>'
      lines: '<start-end>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
    flags: []  # '[unverified]' for claimed, '[derived]' for computed values
    derivation: null  # formula string if flags contains '[derived]', else null
```
After the YAML block, include Markdown prose section:
## Metrics Context
1-2 paragraphs summarizing types of metrics found, quality distribution (configured vs counted vs measured vs claimed), and notable gaps. Call out specific M::... IDs for the most impactful metrics that Stage 3 should prioritize for resume bullet quantification.
page_notes.author: szzhu

page_notes.content:
SOURCE TYPE DEFINITIONS:
  'configured': Parameters explicitly set in code, config files, or launch files — PID gains, control rates, tolerances, thresholds, buffer sizes, timeouts, filter parameters, queue depths. Citation: the line where the value is assigned or the config file entry.
  'counted': Values determinable by counting code artifacts — number of source files, functions/methods, test cases, ROS topics, ROS nodes, modules, API endpoints, state-machine states. Citation: null for whole-repo counts; specific file for per-module counts.
  'measured': Benchmark results, test outputs, profiling data found in log files, CI artifacts, test result files, or performance comments with commit-hash references. Citation: the file containing the measurement. If from a code comment citing test results, confidence: medium.
  'claimed': Numbers stated in README, documentation, paper references, comments, or commit messages without direct code or test backing. ALWAYS add '[unverified]' to flags array — no exceptions. Citation: the document file.

METRIC QUALITY RULES:
  (1) Extract EXACT values — 47.3 stays 47.3, not '~50'. 0.001 stays 0.001, not '< 0.01'.
  (2) Always include units. Use SI where applicable. If dimensionless, use 'dimensionless' or the domain convention (e.g., 'count').
  (3) Each metric MUST have a context field of ≥10 words explaining what the number means in the system.
  (4) Derived values: if you compute a value (e.g., scan_coverage_pct = files_scanned / files_in_repo × 100), flag as '[derived]' and show the formula in derivation field.
  (5) claimed metrics ALWAYS flagged '[unverified]' — no exceptions.
  (6) DEDUPLICATION: one metric per unique (value, unit, context) triple. If the same parameter appears in two files, use the primary definition source only.
  (7) Target: 5-25 metrics per repo. Fewer high-confidence metrics beat many uncertain ones. Do NOT pad with trivial counts.
page_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Dependency Graph
pages.purpose: Extract the runtime communication architecture. For ROS/ROS2: nodes, topics (with message types and QoS), services, actions, TF frames, launch composition. For non-ROS: inter-process communication, API boundaries, event buses, message queues, shared-memory interfaces, plugin architectures. Key output: pipeline_chains (P{nn}) — end-to-end data flow paths that Stage 3 uses for system-level resume bullets. CONDITIONAL: If the repo is a single-process library with NO IPC, SKIP this page entirely and note in Skill Inventory prose: 'Dependency Graph omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Dependency Graph:
```yaml
schema_version: '3.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

# graph_type classifies the dominant IPC paradigm of the system.
# Choose the single best-fit label; mixed systems: pick the outermost/primary boundary.
graph_type: <ros1|ros2|microservice|multi_process|plugin|hybrid>

# RUNTIME COMMUNICATION GRAPH
# Model the system as a directed graph of runtime processes (nodes) and
# their communication channels (edges). This is NOT a dependency/import graph —
# it captures what talks to what at runtime and HOW.

nodes:
  - id: '<short_slug>'               # unique within this file; used in edges src/dst
    name: '<display name>'
    type: <process|thread|ros_node|ros2_node|container|plugin|library_boundary>
    entrypoint: '<path to main source file or entrypoint symbol>'
    language: '<C++|Python|Rust|Go|Java|...>'
    responsibility: '<2 factual sentences: what runtime role this node plays>'
    lifecycle: <persistent|on_demand|periodic>   # persistent=long-running daemon, on_demand=spawned per request, periodic=cron/timer
    skill_refs: ['S::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}']

edges:
  - id: '<short_slug>'               # unique; referenced by pipeline_chains.stages.edge
    src: '<node id>'                  # sender / caller / publisher
    dst: '<node id>'                  # receiver / callee / subscriber
    mechanism: <socket|shared_memory|pipe|http|grpc|rest|message_queue|ros_topic|ros_service|ros_action|plugin_api|dbus|signal|file_ipc|mmap>
    # --- data shape ---
    data_format: '<what crosses the channel: e.g., sensor_msgs/LaserScan, protobuf SensorFrame, raw bytes, JSON, numpy ndarray>'
    schema_ref: '<path to .proto / .msg / .srv / .json-schema / .idl file, or null>'
    # --- flow characteristics ---
    direction: <unidirectional|bidirectional|request_reply|publish_subscribe|stream>
    frequency_hz: <float|null>         # null if event-driven or unknown
    payload_size_bytes: '<estimate or null>'
    # --- reliability ---
    reliability: <guaranteed|best_effort|at_least_once|at_most_once|exactly_once|null>
    ordering: <ordered|unordered|null>
    # --- latency (fill if derivable from code/config, else null) ---
    latency_budget_ms: <float|null>
    # --- quality of service (ROS2 / message queues) ---
    qos_profile: '<e.g. reliable/transient_local, or null for non-ROS>'
    queue_depth: <int|null>
    # --- provenance ---
    citation:
      file: '<repo-relative path>'
      lines: '<start-end>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>

# TF tree: ROS/ROS2 only; omit entirely for non-ROS repos
tf_tree:
  - parent_frame: '<frame_id>'
    child_frame: '<frame_id>'
    publisher_node: '<node id>'
    static: <true|false>
    citation:
      file: '<path>'
      lines: '<start-end>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>

# launch_composition: ROS/ROS2 only; omit for non-ROS repos
launch_composition:
  - launch_file: '<path>'
    nodes_launched: ['<node id>']
    parameters_loaded: ['<param file path>']

# pipeline_chains: end-to-end data flows from external stimulus to actuation/output.
# Each stage now references a specific edge id to make the data path unambiguous.
pipeline_chains:
  - id: 'P{nn}'
    name: '<8 word descriptive pipeline name>'
    description: '<2 sentences: end-to-end data flow and its purpose>'
    trigger: '<what initiates this pipeline: timer, external event, hardware interrupt, user request, ...>'
    stages:
      - node: '<node id>'
        edge: '<edge id | null for first stage>'   # incoming edge that delivers data to this stage
        role: '<5 words: what this stage does>'
        latency_contribution_ms: <float|null>
    input: '<data or stimulus entering the pipeline>'
    output: '<final result or actuation>'
    latency_budget_ms: <float|null>
    skill_refs: ['S::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}']
    evidence_refs: ['E::{taxonomy_ref}:{skill_name}:{evidence_key}']
```
After the YAML block, include Markdown prose sections:
## System Topology
2-3 paragraphs describing the overall runtime communication architecture — node count, dominant IPC mechanisms, notable design patterns (e.g., shared-memory zero-copy for sensor data, gRPC for cross-language service boundaries, message queues for decoupled pipeline stages).
## Communication Mechanism Summary
A concise table listing each distinct mechanism used, the number of edges using it, direction pattern, and the primary design rationale. Example columns: Mechanism | Edge Count | Direction Pattern | Rationale.
## Critical Data Paths
1-2 paragraphs identifying which pipeline_chains are most architecturally significant and why. Reference specific P{nn} IDs and the specific edge ids that are the most latency-critical hops.

CONDITIONAL INCLUSION: Generate this page ONLY when the repo exhibits inter-process communication. Positive indicators: ROS/ROS2 node definitions (ros::init, rclcpp::init), launch files (roslaunch XML, ros2 launch Python), multiple main() entry points, socket/pipe/shared-memory usage, HTTP/gRPC server setup, plugin loading (pluginlib, dlopen), message queue clients (ZMQ, RabbitMQ, Kafka, NATS). For single-process libraries (one main(), no IPC), SKIP entirely and note omission in Skill Inventory.

GRAPH TYPE SELECTION:
  'ros1': catkin workspace, roscpp/rospy imports, roslaunch XML, package.xml with catkin build_depend
  'ros2': colcon workspace, rclcpp/rclpy imports, ros2 launch Python files, ament build type
  'microservice': HTTP/gRPC/REST between separately deployable services, Docker Compose or K8s manifests
  'multi_process': IPC (pipes, shared memory, sockets, MPI) between co-deployed processes
  'plugin': dynamic loading (pluginlib, dlopen, importlib, MEF) as the primary composition mechanism
  'hybrid': two or more of the above paradigms are co-equal; document the dominant paradigm first in System Topology prose

NODE TYPE SELECTION:
  'process': OS-level process with its own address space (most common for non-ROS)
  'thread': intra-process thread with shared memory but distinct communication role (use sparingly; only when threads are the architectural unit, e.g., a dedicated I/O thread)
  'ros_node' / 'ros2_node': ROS process unit
  'container': Docker/OCI container boundary
  'plugin': dynamically loaded shared-library unit with its own interface contract
  'library_boundary': cross-language FFI or C ABI boundary acting as a runtime IPC point

EDGE MECHANISM REFERENCE:
  socket            — TCP/UDP/Unix-domain sockets; raw byte streams or datagrams
  shared_memory     — mmap / POSIX shm / boost::interprocess; zero-copy within a host
  pipe              — anonymous or named pipe (FIFO); unidirectional byte stream
  http              — HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2 without gRPC framing
  grpc              — gRPC (HTTP/2 + protobuf); specify service name in schema_ref
  rest              — RESTful HTTP API; JSON or XML payload
  message_queue     — broker-based async queue (ZMQ, RabbitMQ, Kafka, NATS, ROS bag replay)
  ros_topic         — ROS1/ROS2 pub/sub (maps to socket or DDS underneath)
  ros_service       — ROS1/ROS2 synchronous request/reply
  ros_action        — ROS2 goal/feedback/result pattern
  plugin_api        — in-process function-call API across a dynamic-library boundary (dlopen, pluginlib)
  dbus              — Linux D-Bus IPC (system or session bus)
  signal            — OS signal (SIGINT, SIGUSR1, etc.) used as a runtime coordination mechanism
  file_ipc          — coordination via filesystem (lock files, named pipes, watch directories)
  mmap              — memory-mapped file used as IPC (distinct from POSIX shm when file-backed and cross-host)

PIPELINE CHAIN RULES:
  (1) Each chain represents a complete processing path from external stimulus to meaningful output or actuation.
  (2) Stages are ordered by data flow direction; each stage 'edge' field names the edge that delivers data INTO that stage.
  (3) A node may appear in multiple chains if it serves multiple pipelines.
  (4) skill_refs should cover ALL skills exercised across all stages — this drives Stage 3 system-level bullet generation.
  (5) evidence_refs point to evidence units documenting key data transformations.
  (6) Target: 3-10 chains per repo. Prioritise architecturally significant paths.
  (7) latency_budget_ms at the chain level is the end-to-end budget; per-stage latency_contribution_ms fields must sum to the chain budget.

TF TREE RULES (ROS only):
  (1) Extract frame relationships from static_transform_publisher, tf2 broadcaster code, URDF/xacro files.
  (2) Mark static: true for fixed transforms (static_transform_publisher, fixed joints).
  (3) If full TF tree exceeds 15 frames, extract only frames referenced by pipeline_chains.

ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULES (graph-specific):
  (1) An edge must be evidenced by actual IPC code (socket bind/connect, shm open, topic advertise, etc.) — do NOT infer edges from import graphs or call graphs alone.
  (2) mechanism must match the actual IPC primitive found in code, not the conceptual intent.
  (3) frequency_hz: null unless a timer, rate object, or comment explicitly sets it.
  (4) latency_budget_ms: null unless derived from a timeout constant, SLA config, or benchmark result in the repo.
  (5) If a communication path exists but its mechanism cannot be determined from available files, record mechanism: 'unknown' and confidence: low.
